mission.title: Install and Configure Kpt on Kubernetes
mission.description: kpt is a package-centric toolchain that automates Kubernetes configuration editing, simplifying the management of Kubernetes platforms and KRM-driven infrastructure. Installing kpt allows you to leverage a powerful CLI for managing configuration as data, enhancing your Kubernetes experience.

steps.title: Create a Namespace for kpt
steps.description:
First, create a dedicated namespace for kpt to keep its resources organized. Run the following command:
```bash
kubectl create namespace kpt
```

steps.title: Install kpt
steps.description:
Next, apply the kpt manifests from the GitHub repository. Use the following command to install version 0.40.0:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kptdev/kpt/releases/download/v0.40.0/kpt-0.40.0.yaml -n kpt
```

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description:
After installation, verify that the kpt pods are running by executing:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kpt
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the kpt pod running in the 'kpt-system' namespace, and you will be able to access the kpt CLI through port-forwarding. You can also verify that resource limits are set correctly on the deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall kpt
uninstall.description:
To uninstall kpt, delete the kpt namespace and all its resources with the following command:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kpt
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade kpt
upgrade.description:
To upgrade kpt to a newer version, first apply the new version's manifest. For example, to upgrade to version 0.41.0, run:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kptdev/kpt/releases/download/v0.41.0/kpt-0.41.0.yaml -n kpt
```

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Running
troubleshooting.description:
If the kpt pods are not running, check the pod status with:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kpt
```
Then, describe the pod for more details:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n kpt
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter issues related to insufficient resources, ensure your cluster has enough CPU and memory allocated. You can check resource usage with:
```bash
kubectl top nodes
```

troubleshooting.title: Network Issues
troubleshooting.description:
If you experience network issues, verify that your cluster's network policies allow communication between pods. Check the network policy with:
```bash
kubectl get networkpolicy -n kpt
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure that you have kubectl installed and configured to communicate with your Kubernetes cluster.
